conjecture_title: Improvement of the bound in Theorem 1.12
paper_title: The regularity method for graphs with few 4-cycles
summary: No follow-up work has been found that improves the bound from $o(\sqrt{n})$ to $n^{1/2-\varepsilon}$ for subsets of $[n]$ without a nontrivial solution to $x_1+x_2+2x_3=x_4+3x_5$. Prendiville (arXiv:2005.03484, 2020) gave an alternative proof of the $o(\sqrt{n})$ result of Conlon--Fox--Sudakov--Zhao with effective (quantitative) bounds and extensions to equations in more variables, but this work predates the 2021 publication and does not establish a polynomial gain. The open question of whether $n^{1/2-\varepsilon}$ holds for some $\varepsilon>0$ remains unresolved.

notes: Prendiville (arXiv:2005.03484, 2020, published Math. Proc. Cambridge Phil. Soc. 2022) provides an alternative proof of the o(sqrt(n)) result and effective bounds, but predates the paper's formal 2021 publication and does not address the polynomial improvement question. No papers after 2021 found that resolve or make progress on the n^{1/2-epsilon} question.
